title: Stenness Hyperspace Terminal
content:
{{Top|ga|leg}} {{SpaceStation |image=[[File:Hyperspaceterminal.jpg]] |name=Stenness Hyperspace Terminal |designer= |manufacturer= |line= |model= |type=[[Spaceport/Legends|Spaceport]][[Tales of the Jedi 3|''Tales of the Jedi'' 3]] |cost= |modifier= |sysmods= |length= |width= |height= |diameter= |mass= |max accel= |mglt= |max speed= |maneuverability= |engine= |hyperdrive= |hdrange= |hdsystem= |poweroutput= |power= |shield gen= |hull= |sensor= |target= |navigation= |avionics= |maincomp= |countermeasures= |armament= |complement= |bays=Multiple, on three levels[[Tales of the Jedi (audio drama)|''Tales of the Jedi'' audio drama]] |escapepods= |crew= |skeleton= |passengers= |capacity= |cargohandling= |consumables= |lifesupport= |communications= |othersystems= |region=*[[Inner Rim/Legends|Inner Rim]][[Tales of the Jedi 3|''Tales of the Jedi'' 3]] *[[The Slice/Legends|The Slice]]''[[The Essential Atlas]]'' places the entirety of grid square O-10 in [[The Slice/Legends|the Slice]]. |sector= |system=Near the [[Ambria system/Legends|Stenness system]] |coordinates=O-10{{SW|url=news/star-wars-the-essential-atlas-online-companion|text=''Star Wars: The Essential Atlas'' Online Companion|int=Star Wars: The Essential Atlas Online Companion}} |xyz= |orbited= |distance= |routes= |role=House and service hyperspace travelers |affiliation=[[Bogga's criminal empire]] |constructed= |destroyed= |retired= |battles=A [[Confrontation on the Stenness Hyperspace Terminal|confrontation]] |owners= |namedcrew= |captains= |registry= |aliases= |population= |interest=Many [[Brothel/Legends|brothels]], on levels 54–1000 |services=Multiple [[Foodstuff/Legends|food]] courts, on four levels |sectors= |modules= }} {{Quote|Welcome to the Stenness Spaceport. Docking facilities are located on levels twenty-three, twenty-four, and twenty-five. Food troughs are located on levels fifty, through fifty-three. Brothels are located on levels fifty-four through one thousand.|Terminal announcer|audio=StennessAnnouncer.flac|[[Tales of the Jedi (audio drama)|''Tales of the Jedi'' audio drama]]}} The '''Stenness Hyperspace Terminal''', also known as the '''Stenness Spaceport''', was a [[Space station/Legends|space station]] located near a [[Hyperspace beacon/Legends|hyperspace beacon]] in the [[Ambria system/Legends|Stenness system]] in the [[Inner Rim/Legends|Inner Rim]]. A multilevel construct located on the [[Mining/Legends|mining]] frontier, the terminal contained many [[Merchant/Legends|vendors]] and traders, with hundreds of levels featuring [[Brothel/Legends|brothels]]. Consequently, a large number of criminals on their way to stealing mining rights from the [[Nessie]]s, the natives of the [[Stenness Node]] region, passed through the station. Stenness-based [[Crime lord/Legends|crime lord]] [[Bogga]] the [[Hutt/Legends|Hutt]] and his [[Bogga's enforcer gang|gang]] also maintained a presence on the station. In [[3999 BBY]], [[Jedi Knight/Legends|Jedi Knight]] [[Andur Sunrider]] and his [[Sunrider family|family]], while on their way to deliver a package of valuable [[Adegan crystal/Legends|Adegan crystals]] to [[Jedi Master/Legends|Jedi Master]] [[Thon]], stopped on the Stenness [[Hyperspace/Legends|Hyperspace]] Terminal. The [[Crystal/Legends|crystals]] piqued the interest of Bogga, who sent three of his enforcers to retrieve them. Andur Sunrider was [[Death/Legends|killed]] in the ensuing [[Confrontation on the Stenness Hyperspace Terminal|struggle]], and his [[Marriage/Legends|wife]] [[Nomi Sunrider|Nomi]] took up his [[Lightsaber/Legends|lightsaber]] and struck down the thugs. Nomi Sunrider then left the Stenness Hyperspace Terminal in her [[Starship/Legends|ship]], the ''[[Lightside Explorer]]'', determined to finish her late husband's delivery. ==Description== {{Quote|What a dreadful place!|Nomi Sunrider, after stepping foot inside the Stenness Hyperspace Terminal|}} The Stenness Hyperspace Terminal[[Tales of the Jedi 4|''Tales of the Jedi'' 4]] was a [[Space station/Legends|space station]] located near the [[Ambria system/Legends|Stenness system]] in the [[Inner Rim/Legends|Inner Rim]], and on the [[Whitebeam Run]] [[Hyperspace route/Legends|hyperspace route]]{{InsiderCite|28|The Horse's Mouth|Straight from the Horse's Mouth: A Guide to the ''Star Wars: Tales of the Jedi'' Universe, Part 3}} in the region known as [[The Slice/Legends|the Slice]]. It was a large, [[Color/Legends|gray]], multilevel structure, with many wings and halls extending from its main frame. The [[Spaceport/Legends|spaceport]] had three levels that contained [[Hangar/Legends|docking bays]], four levels featuring [[Foodstuff/Legends|food]] courts that sold foodstuffs such as [[Stenness lizard pie]], and nine hundred forty-seven levels with [[Brothel/Legends|brothels]]. As the Stenness [[Hyperspace/Legends|Hyperspace]] Terminal had a high number of travelers that passed through, many traders and merchants wandered the halls, trying to sell wares such as [[Novanian grog/Legends|Novanian grog]] [[Alcohol/Legends|liquor]]. ==History== ===Beacon of crime=== {{Quote|Hey boss…see that Jedi with the woman and the kid? I heard 'im talkin' about some ''Adegan crystals'' he's carryin'!
"''Hawr hawr hawr'' Adegan crystals! I have a client who'll pay me a grand sum for those pretties.|Gudb and Bogga|}} [[File:Great_Bogga_the_Hutt.jpg|thumb|right|250px|Bogga the Hutt, who operated on the Stenness Hyperspace Terminal]] In [[3999 BBY]],{{InsiderCite|26|The Horse's Mouth|Straight from the Horse's Mouth: A Guide to the ''Star Wars: Tales of the Jedi'' Universe, Part 1}} dates the events of [[Tales of the Jedi 2|''Tales of the Jedi'' 2]] to [[3999 BBY]]. [[Hyperspace beacon/Legends|hyperspace beacons]] were vital to facilitate hyperspace travel. The beacons were constructed at jump points known to be safe and reliable, guiding the way for [[Starship/Legends|ships]] going into hyperspace.''[[The Essential Atlas]]'' At many of the beacons complex spaceports were built, to house and service the ever-expanding trans-[[The galaxy/Legends|galactic]] traffic. One such beacon was located in the Stenness [[Star system/Legends|system]] in the Inner Rim, and it was at this site that the Stenness Hyperspace Terminal was created. The terminal was located on the [[Mining/Legends|mining]] frontier, and by 3999 BBY many criminals were known to pass through the station on their way to stealing mining rights from the natives of the [[Stenness Node]] region, the [[Nessie]]s. [[Miner/Legends|Miners]] [[Transport/Legends|transported]] [[ore]]s to the terminal on cargo ships made from carapaces of the gigantic [[Colossus wasp/Legends|Colossus wasps]]. In 3999 BBY, [[Jedi Knight/Legends|Jedi Knight]] [[Andur Sunrider]] and his [[Sunrider family|family]], while on their way to deliver a package of valuable [[Adegan crystal/Legends|Adegan crystals]] to the [[Tchuukthai]]''[[Power of the Jedi Sourcebook]]'' [[Jedi Master/Legends|Jedi Master]] [[Thon]], docked at the Stenness Hyperspace Terminal in their ship, the ''[[Lightside Explorer]]''. However, two of Stenness-based [[Crime lord/Legends|crime lord]] [[Bogga]] the [[Hutt/Legends|Hutt's]] [[Bogga's enforcer gang|enforcers]], [[Quanto]] and [[Gudb]], discovered the package's existence through the use of on-station eavesdropping equipment. The two thugs brought the information to Bogga himself, piquing the Hutt's interest. ===Confronting the visitors=== {{Quote|Crawl back to your ''dark side'' pit, scum! Tell your ''Hutt boss'' I'll do the same to him!|Nomi Sunrider to Gudb, after taking down Quanto and Rek onboard the space station|}} [[File:SunridersAtStenness.jpg|thumb|left|250px|The Sunrider family arrives on the Stenness Hyperspace Terminal.]] Bogga assembled a group of three enforcers, consisting of Gudb, [[Rek (enforcer)|Rek]], and Quanto, and sent them to [[Confrontation on the Stenness Hyperspace Terminal|accost]] the Sunriders as they waited for their ship to clear customs. Rek and Quanto began to push around Andur Sunrider's [[Protocol droid/Legends|protocol droid]], [[A-3DO]], drawing the [[Jedi/Legends|Jedi's]] attention; when Sunrider turned his back, Gudb released his pet [[Gorm-worm/Legends|gorm-worm]], [[Skritch]], who [[Death/Legends|killed]] Sunrider with its [[Poison/Legends|poisonous]] bite. As Sunrider's [[Marriage/Legends|wife]], [[Nomi Sunrider|Nomi]] knelt over her [[Gender/Legends|husband's]] corpse, Andur's [[Force ghost/Legends|Force ghost]] appeared and directed Nomi to take up his [[Lightsaber/Legends|lightsaber]] and defend herself from the thugs. Nomi did as she was told, and struck down the thugs with her fallen husband's weapon. Soon afterward, Nomi departed the Stenness Hyperspace Terminal in the ''Lightside Explorer'', committed to finishing the delivery of the Adegan crystals. Around the same time as the Sunrider incident, [[Satal Keto/Legends|Satal]] and [[Aleema Keto]], heirs to the [[Throne/Legends|throne]] of the [[Empress Teta system/Legends|Empress Teta system]], visited the station on their ship, the ''[[Krath Enchanter]]''. The Ketos were en route to the [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic]] [[Capital/Legends|capital]] of [[Coruscant/Legends|Coruscant]], and stopped by to [[Fuel/Legends|refuel]]. As the ship had no cargo, it was cleared to land at Docking Bay Four. ==Inhabitants== {{Quote|The scum of the galaxy pass through this station…on their way to stealing ''mining rights'' from the Nessies.|Andur Sunrider|}} Many travelers passed through the Stenness Hyperspace Terminal, a good amount of them being criminals on their way to stealing mining rights from the Nessies. As such, many traders and merchants were present on the spaceport, hoping to do business with passersby. Local crime lord Bogga the Hutt maintained a presence on the Stenness Hyperspace Terminal, along with several of his enforcers. ==Locations== [[File:ExplorerInFlight.jpg|thumb|right|250px|The ''Lightside Explorer'' flies to the Stenness Hyperspace Terminal.]] The Stenness Hyperspace Terminal had a thousand levels; three of them with docking bays, four had food troughs, and nine hundred forty-seven featured brothels. The space station's docking bays, such as Docking Bay Four, where Satal Keto landed in the ''Krath Enchanter'', were all numbered. There was a room where Bogga the Hutt and his gang stayed while on the terminal, along with a room that contained monitoring equipment that Bogga's enforcers used to track incoming ships and to discern which ones they should hijack. However, the monitoring station was located on the other side of the station from some of the docking bays, such as Docking Bay Six, discouraging Bogga's thugs from hijacking vessels in that area. The station's food courts were bustling and full of [[Sentience/Legends|sentient]] beings, and fountains could be found nearby. ==Behind the scenes== The Stenness Hyperspace Terminal first appeared, albeit unidentified, in the [[Tales of the Jedi 3|third issue]] of the [[Star Wars: Tales of the Jedi (comic series)|''Tales of the Jedi'']] [[Star Wars comic books|comic book]] series, which was written by [[Tom Veitch]], illustrated by [[Janine Johnston]], and published by [[Dark Horse Comics]] on [[December 21]], [[1993]].{{Marvel|url=comics/issue/54568/star_wars_tales_of_the_jedi_1993_3|text=Star Wars: Tales of the Jedi (1993) #3}} It was first identified as the Stenness Hyperspace Terminal in ''Tales of the Jedi''{{'s}} [[Tales of the Jedi 4|fourth issue]]—also written by Veitch and released on [[January 18]], [[1994]]{{DarkHorse|url=Comics/93-141/Star-Wars-Tales-of-the-Jedi-4-of-5|text=Star Wars: Tales of the Jedi 4}}—and identified as the Stenness Spaceport in the [[1995]]{{Amazon|url=Star-Wars-Tales-Tom-Veitch/dp/1570421382|text=Star Wars: Tales of the Jedi}} [[Tales of the Jedi (audio drama)|''Tales of the Jedi'' audio drama]] adaptation, based on the comics written by Veitch and adapted by [[John Whitman]]. ==Appearances== *[[Tales of the Jedi (audio drama)|''Tales of the Jedi'' audio drama]] {{1stID|Stenness Spaceport}} *[[Tales of the Jedi 3|''Tales of the Jedi'' 3]] {{1st}} *[[Tales of the Jedi 4|''Tales of the Jedi'' 4]] {{Mo}} {{1stID|Stenness Hyperspace Terminal}}
